claims.name: Contrastive swing L23 bb
claims.statement: At layer 23 with $|c| = 5\%$, contrastive steering along the eot probe produces a 1.00-point preference swing on benign-benign pairs (n=50).

claims.name: Single task swing L23 bb
claims.statement: At layer 23, single-task steering produces a 0.49-point swing in P(picked the steered task) on benign-benign pairs between $c = -5\%$ (0.005) and $c = +5\%$ (0.497).

claims.name: Single task suppression L23 bb
claims.statement: At layer 23 on benign-benign pairs, $c = -5\%$ drops P(pick the steered task) by 0.49 below the pair-type-matched no-steering baseline (0.5 → 0.005).

claims.name: Single task amplification L23 bb
claims.statement: At layer 23 on benign-benign pairs, $c = +5\%$ raises P(pick the steered task) by -0.00 above the pair-type-matched no-steering baseline (0.5 → 0.497).

claims.name: Contrastive swing L23 hb
claims.statement: At layer 23 with $|c| = 5\%$, contrastive steering along the eot probe produces a 0.94-point preference swing on harmful-benign pairs (n=50).

claims.name: Single task swing L23 hb
claims.statement: At layer 23, single-task steering produces a 0.49-point swing in P(picked the steered task) on harmful-benign pairs between $c = -5\%$ (0.232) and $c = +5\%$ (0.722).

claims.name: Single task suppression L23 hb
claims.statement: At layer 23 on harmful-benign pairs, $c = -5\%$ drops P(pick the steered task) by 0.27 below the pair-type-matched no-steering baseline (0.503 → 0.232).

claims.name: Single task amplification L23 hb
claims.statement: At layer 23 on harmful-benign pairs, $c = +5\%$ raises P(pick the steered task) by 0.22 above the pair-type-matched no-steering baseline (0.503 → 0.722).

claims.name: Contrastive swing L23 hh
claims.statement: At layer 23 with $|c| = 5\%$, contrastive steering along the eot probe produces a 0.93-point preference swing on harmful-harmful pairs (n=50).

claims.name: Single task swing L23 hh
claims.statement: At layer 23, single-task steering produces a 0.50-point swing in P(picked the steered task) on harmful-harmful pairs between $c = -5\%$ (0.232) and $c = +5\%$ (0.73).

claims.name: Single task suppression L23 hh
claims.statement: At layer 23 on harmful-harmful pairs, $c = -5\%$ drops P(pick the steered task) by 0.24 below the pair-type-matched no-steering baseline (0.475 → 0.232).

claims.name: Single task amplification L23 hh
claims.statement: At layer 23 on harmful-harmful pairs, $c = +5\%$ raises P(pick the steered task) by 0.26 above the pair-type-matched no-steering baseline (0.475 → 0.73).
